meta.title: Zero to AI Architect
meta.subtitle: Complete Free Roadmap: Math → Python → ML → Deep Learning → Custom Architectures
meta.description: A curated, structured collection of free resources for building AI models from scratch — now expanded with LLM engineering, efficient AI, MLOps, and additional university-grade textbooks. Math-first, architecture-deep, entirely free.
meta.targetAudience: A Course for ai research path

phases.title: Programming Fundamentals
phases.subtitle: Before the math — learn to think like a programmer
phases.description: Core programming literacy in Python and C, plus the data structures and algorithms every CS program assumes. Nothing in Phase 2 onward works without this.

phases.title: Mathematical Foundations
phases.subtitle: The language of AI
phases.description: Re-learn math from first principles with university-level rigor. Every topic maps directly to a concept in ML/DL.

phases.title: Python for ML/AI
phases.subtitle: Tools of the trade
phases.description: Scientific Python stack — NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, and the mindset of vectorized thinking.

phases.title: Classical Machine Learning
phases.subtitle: Learn before you deep-learn
phases.description: Regression, classification, kernels, trees, ensembles — understand every algorithm mathematically and implement from scratch.

phases.title: Deep Learning Fundamentals
phases.subtitle: Backprop to transformers
phases.description: Build neural networks from scratch. Understand gradient flow, optimization, regularization, and modern architectures.

phases.title: Advanced Architectures
phases.subtitle: Build what doesn't exist yet
phases.description: Transformers, diffusion models, GNNs, SSMs, MoE — study, implement, and experiment with cutting-edge architectures.

phases.title: Research Methodology & Publishing
phases.subtitle: From implementation to original contribution
phases.description: The meta-skills that let you work independently: reading papers critically, designing clean experiments, writing clearly, and knowing where to submit work. This is what turns 'I followed a tutorial' into 'I made an original contribution' — the part that gets you recognized with minimal supervision.

mathematics.sectionSubtitle: University-grade, foreign-lecture-first. JEE gave you the instincts — these courses give you the proofs.

topics.title: Linear Algebra
topics.whyItMatters: Neural networks ARE matrix multiplications. Embeddings, attention, weight tensors — all linear algebra.

resources.name: Linear Algebra — MIT 18.06
resources.description: The gold standard. Strang's geometric intuition for linear algebra is unmatched. 34 lectures. Includes full problem sets and exams.

resources.name: Essence of Linear Algebra
resources.description: Watch BEFORE MIT 18.06. Visual animations build intuition for every concept. Geometric thinking that transforms understanding.

resources.name: Linear Algebra — MIT 18.06SC (Scholar Edition)
resources.description: Enhanced edition with recitation videos that clarify difficult problems.

resources.name: Mathematics for Machine Learning: Linear Algebra
resources.auditNote: Audit for free — all video content available without payment
resources.description: Bridges linear algebra directly to ML. Covers PCA with hands-on notebooks. Practical, ML-first perspective.

resources.name: Introduction to Applied Linear Algebra — VMLS
resources.description: Practical, ML-focused linear algebra. Short, readable, and comes with Python companion notebooks. Perfect bridge between theory and implementation.

resources.name: Linear Algebra — Khan Academy
resources.description: Excellent for brushing up on weak areas or getting a gentler first pass before MIT 18.06. Very interactive.

topics.title: Multivariable Calculus & Analysis
topics.whyItMatters: Backpropagation is the chain rule. Gradient descent is calculus. Jacobians and Hessians define how models learn.
subtopics: Chain rule (THE most important rule in DL)

resources.name: Multivariable Calculus — MIT 18.02
resources.description: Complete multivariable calculus from MIT. Rigorous treatment of gradients, Jacobians, and vector fields — all essential for DL.

resources.name: Essence of Calculus
resources.description: Watch first. Builds pure geometric intuition for derivatives and integrals. The chain rule episode is especially brilliant for DL prep.

resources.name: Mathematics for ML: Multivariate Calculus
resources.description: Directly maps calculus to neural network training. Covers backprop mathematically, Jacobians in chain rules, Taylor approximations in optimization.

resources.name: Calculus — Khan Academy (Full Series)
resources.description: Excellent for brushing up specific topics. Use alongside MIT 18.02 for extra practice on any weak areas.

resources.name: Single Variable Calculus — Khan Academy
resources.description: If multivariable feels too fast, start here. Covers limits, derivatives, and integrals with endless practice.

topics.title: Probability & Statistics
topics.whyItMatters: Every ML model is a probabilistic model. Bayesian thinking, distributions, MLE, KL divergence — probability IS machine learning.
mlConnections: KL Divergence in VAEs and diffusion
subtopics: Common distributions: Gaussian, Binomial, Poisson, Categorical, Beta, Dirichlet
Law of Large Numbers and CLT
Information theory: entropy, cross-entropy, KL divergence, mutual information

resources.name: Probabilistic Systems Analysis — MIT 6.041
resources.description: Tsitsiklis is legendary. This is rigorous, proof-based probability that truly builds the foundation needed for statistical ML.

resources.name: Probability for Computer Scientists — Stanford CS109
resources.description: Probability taught with CS applications in mind. Covers Bayesian networks, MLE, and connects strongly to ML. Notes are excellent.

resources.name: Statistics 110: Probability — Harvard
resources.description: Beautifully taught probability with deep problem-solving intuition. The accompanying free textbook 'Introduction to Probability' is a gem.

resources.name: Bayes Theorem Visual Intuition
resources.description: Single best video to understand Bayes' theorem visually. Watch early in your probability journey.

resources.name: Introduction to Probability (Free Textbook)
resources.description: The companion textbook to Stat 110. Free PDF available. Story-based approach with R/Python code examples. A gem.

resources.name: Machine Learning Crash Course (Google) — Probability Primer
resources.description: Not a full probability course, but excellent for seeing how probability concepts immediately apply to ML. Great sanity check after theory courses.

topics.title: Optimization Theory
topics.whyItMatters: Training an AI model = solving an optimization problem. Convexity, saddle points, learning rates, second-order methods — all optimization.

resources.name: Convex Optimization — Stanford EE364A
resources.format: Video + Slides + Problem Sets + Free Textbook
resources.description: The definitive optimization course. Boyd's textbook is free online. Covers everything from convex sets to duality to SDP. Used by researchers worldwide.

resources.name: Convex Optimization — CMU 10-725
resources.description: CMU's ML-focused optimization course. Notes are exceptionally well-written. Covers proximal methods, coordinate descent, and stochastic methods.

resources.name: Nonlinear Optimization — MIT 15.084J
resources.description: Non-convex and constrained optimization. Important for understanding the loss landscapes of deep networks.

resources.name: Convex Optimization (Free Textbook)
resources.description: The textbook for EE364a. Completely free. The definitive reference for convex optimization in ML.

topics.title: Information Theory
topics.whyItMatters: Cross-entropy loss, KL divergence, mutual information, ELBO — information theory is everywhere in modern AI.
subtopics: Cross-entropy and its use as a loss function

resources.name: Information Theory — MIT 6.441
resources.description: Rigorous information theory. Cover the first 8 lectures for ML-relevant content: entropy, KL divergence, channel capacity.

resources.name: Visual Information Theory
resources.description: The best single introduction to information theory. Interactive visualizations of entropy, KL divergence, and cross entropy. Must-read.

resources.name: Information Theory, Inference, and Learning Algorithms
resources.description: A masterpiece that connects information theory directly to ML algorithms. Free from Cambridge. Covers entropy, coding, Monte Carlo methods, and neural networks.

topics.title: Discrete Math & Algorithms (for AI)
topics.whyItMatters: Graph neural networks need graph theory. Attention is set theory. Dynamic programming underlies RL.
subtopics: Graph theory — nodes, edges, paths, trees
Complexity: O-notation, P vs NP intuition

resources.name: Mathematics for CS — MIT 6.042J
resources.instructor: Prof. Tom Leighton & Marten van Dijk
resources.description: Logic, proofs, induction, graphs, probability — all through a CS lens. The problem sets are legendary.

resources.name: Graph Theory — Reinhard Diestel (Free Electronic Edition)
resources.description: Standard graduate graph theory text. Electronic edition is free. Excellent reference for GNNs and combinatorial ML.

topics.title: Differential Equations & SDEs
topics.whyItMatters: Score-based and diffusion generative models are built directly on stochastic differential equations. Neural ODEs and continuous-time models depend on this too — without it, the diffusion model papers in Phase 5 are unreadable.

resources.name: Differential Equations — MIT 18.03SC
resources.description: Complete ODE course from MIT. Covers first/second order equations, systems, and numerical methods — the foundation before SDEs.

resources.name: Differential Equations — Khan Academy
resources.description: Gentler first pass before MIT 18.03, or good for filling specific gaps.

resources.name: Score-Based Generative Modeling through Stochastic Differential Equations
resources.description: The author of the SDE formulation explains it directly. Read once you have ODE/SDE basics — it's the bridge between this math and the diffusion models in Phase 5.

pythonML.sectionSubtitle: Scientific stack mastery — from arrays to GPU tensors

topics.title: NumPy — Vectorized Computing
topics.description: The backbone of all numerical computing in Python. Everything in ML is NumPy at its core.

resources.name: Python/NumPy Tutorial — Stanford CS231n
resources.description: The best concise NumPy tutorial. Written for DL students. Covers all essential ops with ML context.

resources.name: NumPy Official Docs + Tutorials
resources.description: Use the 'Absolute Beginner' guide then the 'NumPy for MATLAB users' if you have any numerical background.

topics.title: Pandas — Data Wrangling
topics.description: Real-world ML data is messy. Pandas is how you clean, explore, and transform it.

resources.name: Pandas — Kaggle Learn
resources.description: Best free interactive Pandas course. 6 lessons, all in-browser Jupyter. Practical and fast.

topics.title: PyTorch — Deep Learning Framework
topics.description: The primary research and production framework. You will write most of your custom architectures in PyTorch.

resources.name: PyTorch Official Tutorials
resources.description: Start with 'Deep Learning with PyTorch: A 60 Minute Blitz'. Then do 'Learning PyTorch with Examples'.

resources.name: Neural Networks: Zero to Hero — Andrej Karpathy
resources.instructor: Andrej Karpathy (former Tesla AI Director, OpenAI co-founder)
resources.description: Absolute must-watch. Karpathy builds micrograd (autograd engine), makemore (character LM), and GPT from scratch. Teaches you to think like a model architect.

topics.title: JAX — Functional Deep Learning
topics.description: Google's NumPy + autograd + JIT + vmap. Used in cutting-edge research. Growing rapidly.

resources.name: JAX Quickstart (Official)
resources.description: Official JAX docs. Start with the quickstart then move to the JAX 101 tutorial series.

topics.title: Data Visualization
topics.description: You can't understand what you can't see. Matplotlib, Seaborn, and interactive plotting for EDA and model interpretation.

resources.name: Python Data Science Handbook (Visualization Chapters)
resources.description: Comprehensive, free online book. Chapters on Matplotlib and Seaborn are the best free resource for visualization in Python.

resources.name: Matplotlib Official Tutorials
resources.description: Start with 'Pyplot Tutorial' and 'Image Tutorial'. Essential for plotting loss curves, weights histograms, and activation maps.

topics.title: Developer Tools & Environment
topics.description: Jupyter, Git, Linux basics, and the command line. The tooling literacy every self-taught researcher needs.

resources.name: The Missing Semester of Your CS Education
resources.description: MIT's free course on all the tools you actually use: command line, Git, Vim, data wrangling with CLI. Absolutely essential for self-learners.

resources.name: GitHub Skills
resources.description: Learn Git and GitHub by doing, inside GitHub itself. Create repos, branches, pull requests, and merge conflicts with guided bots.

resources.name: Jupyter Notebook: An Introduction (Real Python)
resources.description: Comprehensive free tutorial on Jupyter. Covers installation, magic commands, plotting inline, and best practices for ML experiments.

topics.title: SQL & Data Engineering Basics
topics.description: Real-world ML data lives in databases, not just CSVs. Learn to query, join, and aggregate data before it ever reaches a DataFrame.

resources.name: Intro to SQL & Advanced SQL — Kaggle Learn
resources.description: Free interactive SQL course, in-browser. Pairs naturally with the Pandas course in the same track.

resources.name: SQLBolt
resources.description: Fast, interactive SQL drills. Good for quick syntax reinforcement.

resources.name: Mode SQL Tutorial
resources.description: Thorough, example-driven SQL tutorial that goes further into real analytical queries than most intro courses.

topics.title: Experiment Tracking & Environment Management
topics.description: Once you're running more than one training run, you need a way to track what worked and reproduce it. This is the unglamorous infrastructure that separates organized research from chaos.

resources.name: Weights & Biases — Official Tutorials
resources.description: Free tier is generous. Start logging loss curves and hyperparameters from your very first NumPy neural net milestone onward.

resources.name: Conda User Guide
resources.description: Environment management so your PyTorch/CUDA versions don't silently conflict across projects.

resources.name: Docker Curriculum
resources.description: A genuinely friendly first introduction to Docker. Useful once you start deploying models (Milestone 12).

machineLearning.sectionSubtitle: Understand every algorithm: math-first, implement from scratch, then use the library

courses.name: Machine Learning — Stanford CS229
courses.description: The most comprehensive ML course in existence. Covers linear regression, logistic regression, SVMs, neural nets, EM algorithm, and more — all mathematically. Lecture notes are standalone textbooks.

courses.name: Machine Learning — CMU 10-701
courses.description: PhD-level ML at CMU. More theoretically rigorous than CS229. PAC learning, VC dimension, kernel theory. For those who want depth.

courses.name: Machine Learning — UBC (Nando de Freitas)
courses.description: Nando de Freitas (now at DeepMind) teaches ML with exceptional clarity. Bayesian approach throughout. Bridges classical ML and deep learning beautifully.

courses.name: Scikit-Learn Official User Guide
courses.description: After understanding algorithms mathematically, implement with sklearn. The best ML library documentation that exists.

courses.name: Learning From Data — Caltech
courses.description: Theoretical but incredibly clear. Abu-Mostafa focuses on the *why* behind learning theory. Free textbook 'Learning From Data' accompanies the course. Excellent for understanding generalization.

courses.name: An Introduction to Statistical Learning (ISL)
courses.instructor: Gareth James, Daniela Witten, Trevor Hastie, Robert Tibshirani
courses.format: Free PDF + YouTube Videos + R/Python Labs
courses.description: The most popular ML textbook in the world. Free PDF. Gentle but rigorous coverage of regression, classification, trees, SVMs, unsupervised learning. Python labs available.

courses.name: Machine Learning Crash Course
courses.description: Google's fast, practical introduction. Heavy on TensorFlow but concepts are universal. Great for a quick applied pass before diving into CS229 theory.

deepLearning.sectionSubtitle: From a single neuron to training transformers — implement everything from scratch

courses.name: Convolutional Neural Networks — Stanford CS231n
courses.instructor: Andrej Karpathy, Fei-Fei Li, Justin Johnson
courses.description: THE vision deep learning course. CNNs, backprop, batch norm, transfer learning, detection, segmentation. Assignments include building a full neural network from scratch in NumPy.

courses.name: NLP with Deep Learning — Stanford CS224N
courses.description: The definitive NLP/LLM course. Covers word2vec, LSTMs, attention, transformers, BERT, GPT, pretraining, and more. Assignments are world-class.

courses.name: Deep Learning Specialization
courses.description: Covers NNs, CNNs, RNNs, hyperparameter tuning, and sequence models. More accessible than CS231n. Good for cementing fundamentals.

courses.name: Practical Deep Learning for Coders (fast.ai)
courses.description: Top-down, code-first approach. Build state-of-art models from lesson 1. Complementary to bottom-up courses. Teaches practical intuitions that academic courses miss.

courses.name: Neural Networks for ML — Geoffrey Hinton (Toronto)
courses.description: The Godfather of Deep Learning teaching his own field. Historical and deeply insightful. Covers RBMs, DBNs, backprop from first principles.

courses.name: Deep Learning — NYU (Yann LeCun & Alfredo Canziani)
courses.description: LeCun (Godfather of CNNs) teaches deep learning with strong geometric intuition. All materials free. Uses PyTorch. Covers CNNs, RNNs, VAEs, GANs, and modern topics. Highly underrated.

courses.name: Dive into Deep Learning (d2l.ai)
courses.instructor: Aston Zhang, Zachary C. Lipton, Mu Li, Alexander J. Smola
courses.description: The best interactive DL book. Every chapter has runnable code. Covers everything from MLPs to Transformers to RL. Constantly updated. A complete self-contained curriculum.

courses.name: Neural Networks and Deep Learning
courses.description: A beautiful, gentle introduction that builds intuition from scratch. Nielsen explains backpropagation better than almost anyone. Free online. Perfect companion to video courses.

courses.name: Hugging Face NLP Course
courses.description: The practical standard for NLP and Transformers. Learn to use Transformers library, tokenizers, datasets, and fine-tune BERT/GPT. Essential for applied LLM work.

architectures.sectionSubtitle: Study, implement, and innovate on the architectures that define modern AI

architectureGroups.description: The architecture that unified NLP, vision, audio, and more. Understanding attention is non-negotiable for any AI researcher.
architectures: Vanilla Transformer (Vaswani 2017 — Attention Is All You Need)

resources.name: The Annotated Transformer
resources.description: Full implementation of 'Attention Is All You Need' paper with line-by-line annotation. The single best resource for understanding transformers deeply.

resources.name: Let's build GPT: from scratch, in code
resources.description: Karpathy builds GPT-2 from scratch in PyTorch, live. 2 hours. The best architecture tutorial on the internet. Watch twice.

resources.name: Attention in Transformers — 3Blue1Brown
resources.description: Stunning visual walkthrough of multi-head attention. Watch before implementing.

resources.name: Transformers United — Stanford CS25
resources.description: Guest lectures from researchers building the world's most advanced models. Each lecture covers a different transformer variant or application.

resources.name: Hugging Face Transformers Docs & Tutorials
resources.description: The standard library for pretrained transformers. Tutorials cover BERT, GPT, T5, Vision Transformers, and custom architectures. Essential reference.

architectureGroups.description: VAEs, GANs, diffusion models, flow-matching — the architecture family behind image, audio, and video generation.

resources.name: Deep Generative Models — Stanford CS236
resources.description: The best course on generative models. Covers VAEs, GANs, Flows, Diffusion, and Energy-Based Models with rigorous math.

resources.name: Lilian Weng's Blog (OpenAI)
resources.description: The best technical blog in AI. Every post is a mini-textbook on a key topic: diffusion models, attention, contrastive learning, RLHF, and more. Essential reading.

resources.name: Hugging Face Diffusion Models Course
resources.description: Build diffusion models from scratch with Diffusers library. Covers DDPM, Stable Diffusion, fine-tuning, and custom pipelines. Best free diffusion course available.

architectureGroups.description: For non-Euclidean data: molecules, social networks, code, 3D structures.

resources.name: Machine Learning with Graphs — Stanford CS224W
resources.description: The definitive GNN course. Full lectures on YouTube. Leskovec is the leading graph ML researcher.

resources.name: PyTorch Geometric (PyG) Tutorials
resources.description: The standard library for GNNs in PyTorch. Tutorials cover GCN, GAT, GraphSAGE, and large-scale graph training. Must-use after CS224W.

architectureGroups.description: The new frontier — linear-time sequence models challenging transformers.
architectures: S4 — Structured State Space Sequence Models

resources.name: Efficiently Modeling Long Sequences with Structured State Spaces (S4 Paper)
resources.description: The foundational SSM paper. Read after understanding RNNs and convolutions.

resources.name: Mamba: Linear-Time Sequence Modeling with Selective State Spaces
resources.description: The Mamba paper. Introduces selective SSMs. Implement alongside reading.

architectureGroups.description: Training agents, RLHF for LLMs, game-playing AI, robotics control.
architectures: RLHF — Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback

resources.name: Reinforcement Learning — UCL / DeepMind
resources.description: The best RL course. Taught by the researcher who built AlphaGo and AlphaZero. 10 lectures. Rigorous and complete.

resources.name: Reinforcement Learning: An Introduction (Sutton & Barto)
resources.description: The RL bible. Free PDF online. Covers MDPs, Q-learning, policy gradients, function approximation — everything.

resources.name: Spinning Up in Deep RL (OpenAI)
resources.description: OpenAI's guide to deep RL with clean implementations of PPO, SAC, DDPG, TD3. The practical complement to David Silver's theory.

resources.name: Deep Reinforcement Learning Course (Hugging Face)
resources.description: Learn RL by training agents in Unity and PyBullet. Covers Q-Learning, Policy Gradient, PPO, and RLHF for LLMs. Very hands-on with leaderboards.

architectureGroups.description: Training, fine-tuning, aligning, and deploying large language models. RLHF, instruction tuning, parameter-efficient methods, and inference optimization.
architectures: RLHF — Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback

resources.name: The Alignment Handbook (Hugging Face)
resources.description: End-to-end recipes for aligning LLMs. Covers SFT, DPO, ORPO with code. The best free resource for practical RLHF beyond theory.

resources.name: Constitutional AI & RLHF — Anthropic Research
resources.description: Anthropic publishes extensively on RLHF, Constitutional AI, and alignment. Their research blog and papers are essential for understanding modern LLM training.

resources.name: Build a Large Language Model From Scratch (Code)
resources.description: Complete open-source companion code to Raschka's book. Build GPT from scratch, pretrain, fine-tune, and implement RLHF. All code is free even without the book.

resources.name: LLM Training Pipeline Guide (Hugging Face Examples)
resources.description: Hugging Face official examples for pretraining and fine-tuning language models. The ground truth for implementation details.

architectureGroups.description: Understanding not just how to build capable models, but how to understand what they're doing internally and whether they're doing it safely. A fast-growing research area in its own right, and increasingly its own publication track.

resources.name: Transformer Circuits Thread
resources.description: Anthropic's ongoing interpretability research, published openly as it happens. The best place to see what doing real mechanistic interpretability research actually looks like.

resources.name: AI Safety Fundamentals
resources.description: Structured, free curriculum on alignment and safety, with an optional cohort-based version. Good entry point before diving into primary research.

resources.name: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
resources.description: The paper that grounded AI safety as a practical engineering problem rather than pure speculation. Still the right starting point.

resources.name: 200 Concrete Open Problems in Mechanistic Interpretability
resources.description: A literal list of unsolved problems you could pick up and work on — useful once you're ready to find an actual research project rather than just study the field.

architectureGroups.description: Making models faster, smaller, and cheaper without sacrificing accuracy. Critical for deploying AI and running experiments on limited hardware.

resources.name: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models
resources.description: The paper that made fine-tuning 7B+ parameter models accessible to everyone. Read and implement this to understand parameter-efficient fine-tuning.

resources.name: PEFT — Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (Hugging Face)
resources.description: Official docs for LoRA, QLoRA, Prefix Tuning, and more. Includes Colab notebooks. The practical standard for efficient fine-tuning.

resources.name: TinyML — Harvard / edX (Audit Free)
resources.auditNote: Audit for free — full video and lab access
resources.description: Deploy ML on microcontrollers. Covers quantization, pruning, and embedded deployment. Unique perspective on efficiency at the extreme edge.

freeBooksAndPapers.sectionSubtitle: University-grade textbooks available for free — and the papers that changed AI

books.title: Deep Learning
books.description: The DL bible. Part 1 covers all the math you need. Part 2 covers modern DL. Part 3 covers research frontiers.

books.title: Probabilistic Machine Learning: An Introduction
books.description: Modern, Bayesian-first ML textbook. Free PDF. Excellent companion to CS229.

books.title: Probabilistic Machine Learning: Advanced Topics
books.description: Research-level. Covers diffusion models, flows, GNNs, causality. The most up-to-date free ML book.

books.title: Understanding Machine Learning: From Theory to Algorithms
books.description: Theory-first ML. PAC learning, VC dimension, generalization bounds. For those who want to understand why algorithms work.

books.title: Mathematics for Machine Learning
books.description: Bridges math and ML explicitly. Covers linear algebra, calculus, probability through the lens of ML algorithms.

books.title: Dive into Deep Learning
books.description: Interactive textbook with runnable code for every section. Covers the entire DL pipeline from linear regression to transformers. Constantly updated.

books.title: Understanding Deep Learning
books.description: The most up-to-date deep learning textbook (2023). Beautiful explanations of modern architectures, transformers, diffusion models, and GNNs. Free PDF.

books.title: Neural Networks and Deep Learning
books.description: A gentle, intuition-first introduction. Nielsen explains backpropagation better than almost anyone. Free web book with interactive elements.

books.title: An Introduction to Statistical Learning
books.description: ISL is the standard first textbook for statistical ML. Free PDF and Python labs. Covers regression, classification, resampling, trees, SVMs, and unsupervised learning.

books.title: The Hundred-Page Machine Learning Book
books.description: A concise, no-fluff overview of all major ML concepts. The first 100 pages are free on the author's website. Excellent for quick review or first pass.

books.title: Information Theory, Inference, and Learning Algorithms
books.description: Connects information theory, probability, and neural networks. Free from Cambridge University Press. A deep, unified view of learning.

seminalPapers.title: Attention Is All You Need
seminalPapers.significance: Introduced the Transformer — the architecture behind GPT, BERT, and essentially all modern AI

seminalPapers.title: Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition
seminalPapers.significance: Skip connections that enabled training of 100+ layer networks. ResNets are still everywhere.

seminalPapers.title: Batch Normalization
seminalPapers.significance: Enabled training of very deep networks by normalizing activations

seminalPapers.title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners (GPT-3)
seminalPapers.significance: Showed that scale + pretraining enables emergent few-shot capabilities

seminalPapers.title: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models
seminalPapers.significance: Foundational paper for all modern image/video generation models

seminalPapers.title: Mastering the Game of Go with Deep Neural Networks and Tree Search
seminalPapers.significance: AlphaGo paper — breakthrough in RL + deep learning

seminalPapers.title: Auto-Encoding Variational Bayes
seminalPapers.significance: Introduced VAEs — foundational for generative AI

seminalPapers.title: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models
seminalPapers.significance: Revealed the power-law relationship between model size, data, compute and loss

seminalPapers.title: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models
seminalPapers.significance: Made fine-tuning multi-billion parameter models accessible on consumer GPUs. The foundation of modern efficient LLM adaptation.

seminalPapers.title: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
seminalPapers.significance: InstructGPT / RLHF paper. Established the standard pipeline for aligning LLMs with human preferences.

seminalPapers.title: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision (CLIP)
seminalPapers.significance: Pioneered multimodal learning by training on image-text pairs. Foundation for modern vision-language models and zero-shot classification.

seminalPapers.title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models
seminalPapers.significance: Open-sourced performant foundation models, triggering the open LLM revolution and enabling research outside big tech.

seminalPapers.title: Training Compute-Optimal Large Language Models (Chinchilla)
seminalPapers.significance: Revised scaling laws: model size and training data must scale equally. Changed how the industry trains LLMs.

practiceAndProjects.sectionSubtitle: Build real things. Implement papers. Win competitions.

platforms.name: Kaggle
platforms.description: The best platform to practice ML. Free GPU/TPU notebooks. Competitions with real data. Learn from top solutions.

platforms.name: Papers With Code
platforms.description: Every major AI paper with linked open-source implementations. Browse state-of-the-art benchmarks and find papers to implement.

platforms.name: Hugging Face
platforms.description: The GitHub of ML. Free model hub, datasets, Spaces to deploy models. HF Transformers library is the standard.

platforms.name: Google Colab
platforms.description: Free T4 GPU access. Run all your DL experiments here. Upgrade to Colab Pro for better GPUs when needed.

platforms.name: arXiv
platforms.description: Where all AI research is published first. Follow cs.LG (ML) and cs.AI daily.

platforms.name: Made With ML
platforms.description: Goku Mohandas' legendary free course. Covers the full ML lifecycle: data, training, evaluation, deployment, and monitoring. MLOps for practitioners.

platforms.name: Full Stack Deep Learning
platforms.description: Learn to ship AI projects. Covers data management, experimentation, deployment, and LLM apps. The bridge between research prototypes and production.

platforms.name: LeetCode (Free Tier)
platforms.description: Essential for Python fluency and algorithmic thinking. Do the 'Easy' and 'Medium' arrays/strings problems. Free tier is sufficient.

projectMilestones.title: Implement Linear Regression from scratch
projectMilestones.description: Gradient descent, closed-form solution, vectorized NumPy. No sklearn allowed.

projectMilestones.title: Implement Logistic Regression & Decision Tree from scratch
projectMilestones.description: Build logistic regression with gradient ascent and a CART decision tree with entropy/gini. Compare your implementation to sklearn.

projectMilestones.title: Build a Neural Network from scratch (NumPy only)
projectMilestones.description: Forward pass, backprop, mini-batch SGD, train on MNIST. Achieve 98%+ accuracy.

projectMilestones.title: Train a CNN on CIFAR-10 in PyTorch
projectMilestones.description: Build custom CNN, implement data augmentation, achieve 85%+ accuracy.

projectMilestones.title: Build an LSTM for character-level text generation
projectMilestones.description: Implement a recurrent network (LSTM/GRU) in PyTorch. Train on a book corpus and generate text. Understand vanishing gradients firsthand.

projectMilestones.title: Build a Transformer from scratch
projectMilestones.description: Implement multi-head attention, positional encoding, train a character-level LM. Follow Karpathy's lecture.

projectMilestones.title: Reproduce a published paper
projectMilestones.description: Pick a paper from Papers With Code, implement it from scratch, verify results. Best learning experience in AI.

projectMilestones.title: Fine-tune BERT for a Named Entity Recognition task
projectMilestones.description: Use Hugging Face Transformers to fine-tune a pretrained model. Learn tokenization, datasets, Trainer API, and evaluation metrics.

projectMilestones.title: Train a small GPT on custom data
projectMilestones.description: Collect your own dataset, train a 10M parameter GPT, deploy on Hugging Face Spaces.

projectMilestones.title: Implement LoRA fine-tuning on a small LLM
projectMilestones.description: Take a 1B parameter model, freeze it, and fine-tune with LoRA using PEFT. Understand parameter-efficient adaptation and memory profiling.

projectMilestones.title: Train a Diffusion Model on a custom image dataset
projectMilestones.description: Implement or adapt a DDPM. Train on a small dataset (e.g., Pokemon, anime faces). Understand forward/reverse processes and sampling.

projectMilestones.title: Deploy a model with a FastAPI backend and Gradio frontend
projectMilestones.description: Build an end-to-end ML application. Dockerize it, deploy on Hugging Face Spaces or a free tier cloud provider. Add monitoring.

communityAndBlogs.sectionSubtitle: Stay current. Learn from researchers. Engage with the community.

blogs.name: colah's blog
blogs.author: Chris Olah (Google Brain / Anthropic)
blogs.description: The most beautifully written ML intuition blog. Posts on LSTMs, neural network features, attention, and information theory are essential reading.

blogs.name: Lilian Weng's Blog
blogs.author: Lilian Weng (OpenAI VP Research)
blogs.description: Research-grade explanations of diffusion models, attention, contrastive learning, RLHF. Each post is a mini-survey.

blogs.name: Distill.pub
blogs.author: Multiple ML Researchers
blogs.description: Interactive, peer-reviewed ML articles. The highest quality ML writing on the internet. Covers mechanistic interpretability, attention, and more.

blogs.name: Andrej Karpathy's Blog & Twitter/X
blogs.author: Andrej Karpathy
blogs.description: Deep practical insights from one of the world's best ML practitioners. 'A Recipe for Training Neural Networks' is essential reading.

blogs.name: Sebastian Raschka's Blog
blogs.author: Sebastian Raschka
blogs.description: Deep dives on LLMs, LoRA, pretraining, and ML research. Raschka is one of the best educators for translating papers into practice.

blogs.name: Jay Alammar's Blog
blogs.author: Jay Alammar
blogs.description: The best visual explanations of transformers, BERT, GPT, and tokenization. 'The Illustrated Transformer' is a must-read before implementing attention.

youtubeChannels.name: 3Blue1Brown
youtubeChannels.description: Visual math and ML intuition. Essential for all mathematical foundations.

youtubeChannels.name: Yannic Kilcher
youtubeChannels.description: Best paper explanation channel. Watch his breakdowns of transformers, GANs, and new SOTA papers.

youtubeChannels.name: Andrej Karpathy
youtubeChannels.description: Must-subscribe. 'Zero to Hero' series is the best DL curriculum on YouTube.

youtubeChannels.name: sentdex
youtubeChannels.description: Practical Python ML from reinforcement learning to PyTorch projects.

youtubeChannels.name: Lex Fridman
youtubeChannels.description: Long-form interviews with leading AI researchers — Hinton, LeCun, Bengio, Ng, Karpathy. Essential for perspective.

youtubeChannels.name: StatQuest with Josh Starmer
youtubeChannels.description: Short, crystal-clear explanations of ML and statistics concepts. Perfect for filling gaps before diving into heavy theory.

youtubeChannels.name: Umar Jamil
youtubeChannels.description: Deep technical breakdowns of transformer papers (LLaMA, Mistral, Mixtral) with code walkthroughs. Excellent for advanced architecture study.

youtubeChannels.name: Two Minute Papers
youtubeChannels.description: Stay current with AI research. Quick, exciting summaries of the latest papers in graphics, ML, and robotics.

schedule.daily: CS50P / freeCodeCamp Python -> CS50x C weeks -> MIT 6.006 (start) + VisuAlgo
schedule.milestone: Comfortable with Python OOP, C pointers/memory, and basic DSA (arrays, recursion, complexity)

schedule.daily: 3Blue1Brown (Essence of LA) -> MIT 18.06 lectures -> Problem sets
schedule.milestone: Comfortable with matrix ops, eigenvalues, SVD
Understand gradients, Jacobians, chain rule deeply

schedule.daily: Harvard Stat 110 or MIT 6.041 -> CS109 Stanford
schedule.milestone: Comfortable with distributions, MLE, Bayes theorem, entropy

schedule.daily: MIT 18.03 -> Yang Song's SDE blog post
schedule.milestone: Understand ODEs well enough to read the score-based diffusion SDE formulation

schedule.focus: Python Scientific Stack + SQL + Experiment Tracking
schedule.daily: NumPy -> Pandas -> SQL (Kaggle/SQLBolt) -> Matplotlib -> set up Weights & Biases
schedule.milestone: Vectorized code, broadcasting, basic SQL joins, first logged training run

schedule.daily: 2 lectures/week + implement every algorithm from scratch
schedule.milestone: Implement linear regression, logistic regression, SVM, trees from scratch

schedule.focus: Deep Learning — CS231n + Karpathy Zero to Hero
schedule.milestone: Build CNN, RNN, and GPT from scratch in PyTorch
Implement a full transformer, VAE, and diffusion model

schedule.daily: Transformer Circuits Thread + AI Safety Fundamentals + pick one open problem from Neel Nanda's list
schedule.milestone: Can explain what a circuit/feature is, and have a concrete interpretability problem you could investigate

schedule.daily: 1 paper/week using the three-pass method -> learn LaTeX -> build the reproducibility checklist habit
schedule.milestone: Comfortable reading a paper in under an hour and explaining it to someone else

schedule.daily: Read 1 arXiv paper/day -> Implement 1 paper/month -> Kaggle competitions -> draft toward a JEI or workshop submission
schedule.milestone: Build original contributions, publish, contribute to open source

programmingFundamentals.sectionSubtitle: Start here if you're at zero — Python first, C second

topics.title: Python Basics
topics.description: Core programming concepts in Python — variables, control flow, functions, OOP, file I/O. The prerequisite for every later phase; nothing else in this roadmap works without it.

resources.name: CS50's Introduction to Programming with Python
resources.description: Harvard's dedicated Python course. Rigorous and well-paced; the problem sets force real understanding rather than copy-paste familiarity.

resources.name: Scientific Computing with Python (Full Course)
resources.description: freeCodeCamp's Python curriculum with built-in coding challenges and a certificate track. Good for learning by doing rather than just watching.

resources.name: The Python Tutorial (Official Docs)
resources.description: Use as a reference alongside CS50P/freeCodeCamp rather than a primary course — good for looking up exact syntax.

resources.name: Automate the Boring Stuff with Python
resources.description: Practical, project-based Python. A good second pass after CS50P to build muscle memory through small real scripts.

topics.title: C Programming Basics
topics.description: Low-level fundamentals — pointers, manual memory management, compilation. Won't be used directly in ML code, but builds the intuition that makes tensors, memory layout, and GPU programming click later. Also standard for DSA coursework in most engineering curricula.

resources.name: CS50x (Weeks 0-5: C)
resources.description: The first several weeks of CS50x are a genuinely excellent introduction to C — memory, pointers, data structures. You don't need to finish the whole course, just the C portion.

resources.name: C Programming Full Course
resources.description: freeCodeCamp's long-form C course — a practical companion to CS50's more conceptual treatment.

resources.name: learn-c.org
resources.description: Quick interactive drills for C syntax — useful for reinforcing CS50/freeCodeCamp content, in-browser, no setup required.

topics.title: Data Structures & Algorithms
topics.description: Beyond syntax — the structures (arrays, trees, graphs, hash maps) and algorithmic thinking (recursion, dynamic programming, complexity analysis) every CS program and technical interview assumes, and that the Discrete Math phase later builds on.

resources.name: Introduction to Algorithms — MIT 6.006
resources.description: Rigorous, proof-backed treatment of core data structures and algorithms. The standard reference before any technical assessment.

resources.name: VisuAlgo
resources.description: Watch sorting, trees, graphs, and DP algorithms execute step by step. Use alongside MIT 6.006 to build visual intuition.

resources.name: JavaScript Algorithms and Data Structures (concepts transfer)
resources.description: Built around JS, but the structures and algorithmic thinking transfer directly to Python/C. Good for drilling via small interactive exercises.

researchMethodology.sectionSubtitle: Read critically, write clearly, design clean experiments, know where to submit

topics.title: How to Read Papers Efficiently
topics.description: Papers are not meant to be read start to finish like a textbook. Learn the three-pass method and how to extract what matters quickly, so you can read dozens instead of being stuck on one for a week.
keySkills: Identifying the actual contribution vs. framing

resources.name: How to Read a Paper
resources.description: Short, classic guide introducing the three-pass method for reading research papers efficiently. Read this before reading any other paper on this list.

resources.name: How to Read Research Papers — Andrew Ng
resources.description: Ng's practical advice on building a paper-reading habit and a personal reading list, aimed specifically at people self-studying ML.

topics.title: Scientific Writing & LaTeX
topics.description: Clear writing is a force multiplier — a well-written paper with modest results often gets read more than a poorly written paper with great ones. LaTeX is the standard typesetting tool for every ML venue.
keySkills: Structuring a paper (abstract, intro, method, results)

resources.name: Overleaf — Learn LaTeX in 30 Minutes
resources.description: Overleaf is free and is what most ML papers are actually written in. This tutorial gets you writing a real document immediately, no local install needed.

resources.name: How to Write a Great Research Paper
resources.description: One of the most widely recommended talks on paper writing in all of CS. Watch before you write your first draft, not after.

resources.name: Writing in the Sciences
resources.auditNote: Audit for free — all video content available
resources.description: General scientific writing course, not ML-specific, but the principles of clear technical prose transfer directly.

topics.title: Experimental Design & Reproducibility
topics.description: A result nobody can reproduce, including you in six months, isn't a result. Build the habits that make your work trustworthy and your own — this is also what reviewers actually check for.
keySkills: Setting up baselines before claiming improvement

resources.name: ML Reproducibility Checklist
resources.description: The actual checklist top ML conferences use to evaluate reproducibility. Apply it to your own milestone projects before you ever submit anything.

resources.name: The Turing Way
resources.description: A genuinely excellent, free, community-maintained guide to reproducible, ethical, and collaborative research practices.

topics.title: Where & How to Publish as an Independent Researcher
topics.description: Most major ML venues (NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR) run workshop tracks that explicitly welcome students and independently-affiliated authors and don't require a university lab. There are also venues built specifically for pre-university and early researchers. Start there before aiming at a main conference track.
keySkills: Identifying workshop tracks open to independent submissions

resources.name: Journal of Emerging Investigators
resources.description: A real, peer-reviewed journal specifically for middle and high school student research, with working scientists as reviewers and mentors. A genuinely achievable first publication target.

resources.name: OpenReview
resources.description: The platform most major ML conferences and workshops use for submission and review. Browse past workshop submissions to calibrate what a submittable paper actually looks like.
